currency.name: Turkish lira (TRY)
currency.note: Currency in free fall. Inflation 60%+ (officially, actually higher). Erdogan's unorthodox policies destroyed value. Savings evaporate.

crypto.note: High crypto adoption due to lira collapse. Many use crypto and stablecoins to preserve value. Regulated but government wary. Tax treatment unclear.

article:
🧭 **Overview**
Turkey (Türkiye officially since 2022) straddles Europe and Asia, known for Istanbul (Bosphorus, Hagia Sophia, Grand Bazaar), Ottoman legacy, Cappadocia, Mediterranean beaches, and complex politics under Erdogan's increasingly authoritarian rule (since 2003). Istanbul is megacity (16M+); Ankara is capital; Izmir is Aegean coast; Antalya is tourism. The country offers rich history, diverse landscapes, food culture, and strategic location. However, economic crisis (lira collapsed 80%+, inflation 60%+, unorthodox monetary policy), Erdogan's authoritarianism, refugee burden (3.6M Syrians), earthquakes (2023 killed 50k+), and political polarization create serious challenges.

👥 **People & vibe**
With roughly 85 million people, Turkey is ethnically Turkish (~70-75%), Kurdish (~18-20%, southeast), Arab, Circassian, others. Turkish is language; Kurdish is spoken in southeast; English in tourism/business. Islam (Sunni 80%, Alevi 15%) dominates but secularism (Atatürk's legacy) vs Islamism (Erdogan's AKP) divides society. The culture emphasizes hospitality (çay tea culture), honor, family, and polarization (secular vs religious). Turks are warm, proud, nationalist. The vibe is chaotic energy. Istanbul is Europe meets Asia; Ankara is bureaucracy; coast is tourism; southeast is Kurdish/conservative; rural Anatolia is traditional.

[[MAP]]

🌦️ **Climate & landscape**
Climate varies dramatically: Istanbul has oceanic (hot summers, cold winters); Mediterranean coast (Antalya — hot, dry summers); inland Anatolia (Ankara — continental, harsh winters); southeast (hot). The landscape includes Bosphorus strait, Cappadocia fairy chimneys, Mediterranean/Aegean coasts, mountains (Eastern Anatolia), and diverse ecosystems. Natural beauty is extraordinary. Earthquakes are major threat (North Anatolian Fault — 2023 quake killed 50k+). Air quality in cities is poor.

🏠 **Housing & settling in**
Istanbul (Kadıköy, Beşiktaş, Şişli), Antalya, Bodrum attract expats/retirees. Expect negotiable terms. Rents are cheap in dollar terms (lira collapse): ₺15k-40k/month ($470-1,250). Quality varies — modern apartments exist; old buildings lack maintenance. Earthquake risk means building quality matters. Registration required. Many foreigners bought property (citizenship-by-investment program — $400k property). Currency collapse devastated those with lira savings. Security concerns in southeast (Kurdish conflict).

💼 **Work & economy**
The economy is services, manufacturing (automotive, textiles), agriculture, tourism, and construction. For foreigners, opportunities exist in teaching English, tourism, tech (growing startup scene), or remote work. Work permits require employer sponsorship. Salaries in lira are worthless (inflation 60%+, lira collapsed). Many negotiate dollar salaries. Unemployment is 10%+ (youth 20%+). Erdogan's unorthodox monetary policy (cutting rates during inflation) destroyed economy. Starting a business is feasible but currency risk is extreme. Brain drain accelerates.

🛂 **Visa & entry**
E-visa available for most nationalities (90 days in 180 days, $50-100). For longer stays, work permits, student visas, or residence permits (property ownership, sufficient income) available. Citizenship-by-investment program ($400k property purchase) is popular. The process is bureaucratic. Turkish proficiency helps. Permanent residence leads to citizenship eligibility.

🏥 **Healthcare**
Healthcare is two-tier. Public hospitals are overcrowded. Private hospitals offer good care at affordable prices (in dollars). Medical tourism attracts patients (hair transplants, dental, cosmetic surgery). Doctors are well-trained. Life expectancy is ~78 years. International insurance recommended. Prescription drugs are affordable. 2023 earthquake exposed healthcare system limits.

🚗 **Transport & mobility**
Istanbul has metro, trams, buses, ferries, Metrobus — extensive but crowded. Traffic is terrible (3rd-worst globally). Most expats use public transport or taxis. Intercity buses are extensive and comfortable. The country is large — Istanbul to Antalya is 700km (8hr bus, 1hr flight). High-speed trains connect some cities. Domestic flights are affordable. Istanbul Airport is massive hub. Driving is chaotic and aggressive.

🍛 **Food note (national dish)**
The national dish is **Kebab** (döner, Adana, İskender — regional varieties) but Turkish cuisine is incredibly diverse: **Meze** (small plates), **Pide** (Turkish pizza), **Börek** (savory pastries), **Baklava** (dessert). Turkish food is Ottoman legacy — influenced by Balkan, Middle Eastern, Central Asian, Mediterranean cuisines. Çay (tea) culture is constant.

🔎 **Bottom line**
Turkey suits retirees with dollar income, digital nomads, history enthusiasts, and those seeking affordable bridge between Europe/Asia. Pros: rich history (Istanbul, Ephesus, Cappadocia), diverse landscapes, food culture, affordability (lira collapse), and strategic location. Cons: economic crisis (lira collapsed 80%+, inflation 60%+, savings evaporate), Erdogan's authoritarianism (jails journalists, controls media, rigged elections), earthquakes (2023 killed 50k+), refugee tensions, and political polarization. Istanbul is vibrant chaos; coast is beach tourism; Cappadocia is wonder. Best for those with hard currency income and comfortable with authoritarian governance and economic instability. The 2023 earthquake exposed corruption (building codes ignored). If you can handle economic chaos and autocracy for history and affordability, Turkey delivers.

Expat Score — 6.0 / 10

national_dish.name: Kebab / Meze / Börek
national_dish.note: Grilled meat, small plates, or savory pastries — Ottoman legacy cuisine. Balkan/Middle Eastern/Central Asian/Mediterranean fusion. Çay culture.

lgbt_acceptance.note: Homosexuality is legal but social attitudes are hostile. No protections. Pride events banned. Erdogan's government is conservative. Istanbul has underground scene. Discretion essential.

personality.name: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk
personality.story: Founder of modern Turkey (1923), abolished Ottoman Caliphate, secularized state, modernized society (alphabet, women's rights, education). Father of Turks (Atatürk). His secular legacy is being eroded by Erdogan's Islamism. Criticizing Atatürk is criminal offense.

cards.title: 💸 Lira collapse = economic disaster
cards.text: Turkish lira lost 80%+ value since 2018. Inflation 60%+ officially (actually higher). Erdogan's unorthodox policy: cuts interest rates during inflation (opposite of economics). Savings evaporate. Middle class crushed. People buy dollars, crypto, gold. It's economic madness.

cards.title: 👔 Erdogan = authoritarian turn
cards.text: President/PM since 2003. Initially reformist, now autocrat. Jails journalists (Turkey jails most journalists globally), controls media, rigged referendum (2017 — presidential powers), purged military/judiciary (2016 coup attempt pretext). Opposition exists but repressed. Democracy is facade.

cards.title: 💔 2023 Earthquake = 50k+ dead
cards.text: Feb 2023 earthquake (7.8 magnitude) hit southeast killed 50,000+. Thousands of buildings collapsed — construction codes ignored, corruption allowed substandard buildings. Erdogan's response was criticized. Earthquake exposed governance failures. Aftershocks continue. Trauma is profound.

cards.title: 🏙️ Istanbul = Bosphorus bridge
cards.text: Only city spanning two continents (Europe and Asia). Bosphorus strait divides. Hagia Sophia (537 AD, Byzantine church → mosque → museum → mosque again 2020). Grand Bazaar. 16M+ people. Traffic is nightmare. It's cultural, historical, chaotic wonder.

cards.title: 🇸🇾 Syrian refugees = 3.6M burden
cards.text: Turkey hosts 3.6M Syrian refugees (most globally). Initial generosity turned to resentment — economic strain, job competition, cultural tensions. Erdogan uses refugees as leverage vs Europe (threatens to 'open gates'). Integration failed. Many want refugees to leave.

cards.title: 🎈 Cappadocia = fairy chimneys
cards.text: Volcanic landscape with fairy chimney rock formations, underground cities (early Christians hid from Romans), cave hotels, and hot air balloons. It's otherworldly beautiful. Tourism is major draw. Göreme is UNESCO site. Balloon rides at sunrise are iconic.
